items.question: What are train, validation, and test sets used for?
items.answer: Train fits model parameters; validation compares models and hyperparameters during development; test provides a final held-out generalization estimate after selection is complete.

items.question: Why not tune hyperparameters on the test set?
items.answer: Each test-set comparison leaks information into model selection — the test partition stops being unseen and the reported test metric becomes optimistically biased.

items.question: When should you use stratified splitting?
items.answer: For classification (especially imbalanced targets) when you need each partition to preserve approximate class proportions so validation and test metrics are meaningful.

items.question: How do you create a 70/15/15 split with sklearn?
items.answer: First train_test_split with test_size=0.3 and stratify=y, then split the 30% remainder 50/50 with stratify again — yielding 70% train, 15% val, 15% test.

items.question: What is the difference between a random split and a temporal split?
items.answer: Random (stratified) splits shuffle i.i.d. rows; temporal splits assign earlier periods to train and later periods to validation/test — required when time order matters and shuffling would leak future data into training.

items.question: Why must preprocessing be fit on training data only?
items.answer: Fitting scalers or encoders on validation/test rows leaks their statistics into training — held-out metrics overstate generalization because those rows are no longer truly unseen.
